texto: [Measure for Measure] leaves me with the sense that life is all there is, so we might as well live it as best we can; that being human is not a given something we have to strive for. That the reason we are here is to live and that this involves making many difficult judgements.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Roger Allam sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “[Measure for Measure] leaves me with the sense that life is all there is, so we might as well liv…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a vida, measure-for-measure, roger-allam, útil em momentos de roger-allam e shakespeare.

frase_original: [Measure for Measure] leaves me with the sense that life is all there is, so we might as well live it as best we can; that being human is not a given something we have to strive for. That the reason we are here is to live and that this involves making many difficult judgements.
